# The in-app code-execution / test-runner slice's shape — the sandbox for running arbitrary learner code, the "Run tests" route + affordance, the `attempt_questions` test-result columns, and (optionally) displaying the test suite read-only on the take surface

**Status:** Open
**Surfaced:** 2026-05-12 (TASK-016 / ADR-040 / ADR-041) — TASK-016 makes the Question's test suite *exist* (generated by `question_gen`, persisted in `questions.test_suite`); the *next* slice makes the learner *run* it against their code, in-app. That slice was not needed to resolve TASK-016's decisions, so it is captured here rather than predicted in an ADR. (TASK-016's ADRs explicitly do **not** decide it — ADR-040 §Scope and ADR-041 §Scope both defer it here.)

**Decide when:** the in-app-test-runner slice's `/design` (the next Quiz-pillar task after TASK-016 — the conformant order is: Questions-carry-tests (TASK-016) → **in-app code-execution / test-runner (this slice)** → grading (now keyed off test results per the amended §8) → replay-+-fresh composition → TTS; see ADR-040 §My recommendation vs the user's apparent preference for why the in-app runner is load-bearing for grading and the roadmap memory's stated order is stale). Forced then; not before.

## Question

The 2026-05-12 manifest amendment added §6 "**Code is written, run, and tested within the application.** The learner answers a Question by writing code, running it, and running tests against it — all inside the application's own interface" and tightened §8 (Quiz Attempt "the in-app test results for each response"; Grade correctness "determined by whether the learner's code passed the Question's tests"). TASK-016 ships the precondition — the Question carries a test suite (a runnable test-code string in `questions.test_suite`, ADR-040/ADR-041) — but it runs nothing. This slice has to decide:

1. **The code-execution sandbox / isolation story.** Running *arbitrary learner-submitted code* (and the LLM-generated test suite against it) is a real security/isolation problem — the learner's code can be anything (infinite loops, fork bombs, filesystem writes, network calls, resource exhaustion). The decision: a subprocess with `resource` limits (CPU time, memory, no network) + a timeout + a restricted working directory? A container (`docker run --network=none --read-only ...`)? A WASM/`pyodide`-style in-browser sandbox (the learner's code never leaves the browser — but then the test results aren't trusted by the server for grading)? A dedicated jail (`nsjail`, `firejail`, `bubblewrap`)? — this is the substantial ADR of the slice; the project is single-user, single-machine, locally-run, no remote deployment (manifest §5) — which *lowers* the threat model (the attacker is the author, who can already run anything on their own machine) but doesn't eliminate the *robustness* need (a learner's infinite loop shouldn't hang the app; a learner's `rm -rf` shouldn't touch `content/latex/` or `data/notes.db`). The languages the test suites target matter (C++ needs a compiler step; Python is an interpreter step) — the sandbox has to handle whichever languages `question_gen`'s prompt (ADR-040) tells the LLM to emit (currently the prompt steers toward C++ implementations; the runner has to compile-and-run C++ test files).
2. **The "Run tests" route + affordance on the take surface.** ADR-038's `quiz_take.html.j2` has, per `in_progress` Question, a `prompt` + a `<textarea name="response_{question_id}">` for the learner's code. This slice adds a "Run tests" affordance — but is it synchronous (the route runs the sandbox and returns the results in the response — bounded by a timeout)? Or out-of-band like generation/grading (a "Run tests" click records a request, an out-of-band runner processes it, the results show on the next page load — mirroring ADR-037's generation processor)? Manifest §6's "the learner answers a Question by writing code, running it, and running tests against it — all inside the application's own interface" reads as wanting a *responsive* run-and-see-results loop (you write code, click Run, see pass/fail) — which tensions with the out-of-band pattern the AI surfaces use (but the AI surfaces are out-of-band because of MC-4 "AI work is asynchronous from the learner's perspective" — *running a test suite is not AI work*, so MC-4 doesn't bind it; a synchronous test run with a timeout may be the right call here, distinct from the AI-generation/grading async pattern). The slice's `/design` decides; it should reason carefully about whether MC-4 applies (it doesn't — running tests is not an AI-driven feature) and whether §6's "all inside the application's own interface" wants synchronous responsiveness.
3. **The `attempt_questions` test-result columns.** ADR-033's `attempt_questions` table has `response` (the learner's code), `is_correct` (NULL until graded), `explanation` (NULL until graded). §8 Quiz Attempt now says it carries "the in-app test results for each response" — so this slice adds the test-result storage: a `test_passed INTEGER` (1/0 — did the test suite pass)? A `test_output TEXT` (the runner's stdout/stderr — what the learner sees)? A `test_run_at TEXT`? — additive nullable `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN`s (ADR-022's migration story; mirroring ADR-037's `quizzes.generation_error`). Note the relationship to `is_correct`: §8 Grade says correctness is "determined by whether the learner's code passed the Question's tests" — so `is_correct` (set by the grading slice) becomes a *function of* the test-result columns (set by this slice), not a free AI judgment; this slice produces the raw test result, the grading slice reads it. (The slice's `/design` decides whether `is_correct` is set *by the runner* — "the tests passed ≡ correct" — or *by the grading slice* reading the test result — the latter keeps the grading slice's "compute the Grade aggregate" job intact and lets it add the per-Question *explanation* alongside; either is defensible.)
4. **(Optional) Displaying the test suite read-only on the take surface.** Should the learner *see* the test suite they're being tested against (so they know the function signature, the edge cases) — a read-only `<pre>` block per Question on `quiz_take.html.j2`? Or is the test suite hidden (the learner implements from the `prompt` alone, the tests are the hidden verification)? — a UX call; manifest §6 says "writing code, running it, and running tests against it" which is consistent with the learner *seeing* the tests (you can't meaningfully "run tests against your code" without knowing what they test). The slice's `/design` decides; if it shows the test suite, that's a `quiz_take.html.j2` change (a `.quiz-take-test-suite` block in `quiz.css` — ADR-008/ADR-039).
5. **(Optional) The per-Section `ready`-entry caption on the Lecture page.** ADR-040 left the `.section-quiz` block's `ready` entry unchanged ("Ready" + "Take this Quiz" — ADR-038) because a caption mentioning tests before the runner exists risks implying the learner can run them. This slice — which *gives* the test suite a learner-facing role — owns the caption call (e.g. "Ready — N Questions, write code and run tests in-app"); if changed it reuses `section-quiz-*` in `lecture.css` (ADR-008).

## Options known

These are sketches, not commitments — the slice's `/design` decides:

- **The sandbox:** a subprocess with `resource.setrlimit` (CPU/memory/no-`fork`) + a `subprocess.run(timeout=...)` + a restricted temp working directory (the simplest; matches the single-user/single-machine threat model; the implementer compiles C++ to a temp binary, runs it under the limits) — vs. a container (`docker run --network=none --read-only --memory=... --cpus=...` with the learner's code + the test suite mounted in; more isolation, but adds Docker as a dependency the project doesn't have, tensioning with the "no remote deployment / locally-run" simplicity ceiling) — vs. `bubblewrap`/`nsjail`/`firejail` (a Linux-native jail; more isolation than a bare subprocess, fewer dependencies than Docker; Linux-only — fine, the dev environment is Linux) — vs. a WASM/`pyodide` in-browser sandbox (the learner's code never reaches the server — but then the server can't trust the test result for grading, which §8 Grade needs; ruled out for the grading-feeding path, though it could be a *preview* layer). The architect's forecast leans **a subprocess with `resource` limits + a timeout + a restricted temp dir** (simplest; the single-user threat model is "the author can already run anything on their own machine"; the *robustness* need — don't hang the app, don't touch `content/latex/` or `data/notes.db` — is met by the limits + timeout + a temp working dir away from those paths) — but this is the slice's biggest decision and deserves a real `/design`; a `bubblewrap`-style jail is a reasonable upgrade if the bare subprocess proves too leaky.
- **The "Run tests" mechanism:** synchronous (the route runs the sandbox with a timeout, returns the results in the response — a responsive write→run→see loop, which §6 reads as wanting; MC-4 doesn't bind it — running tests is not AI work) — vs. out-of-band (a "Run tests" click records a request; an out-of-band runner processes it; the results show on the next page load — mirrors ADR-037 but for a non-AI task, which is the wrong pattern to copy here). The architect's forecast leans **synchronous with a timeout** (the responsive loop §6 wants; MC-4 doesn't apply; the timeout bounds the cost).
- **The `attempt_questions` test-result columns:** `test_passed INTEGER` + `test_output TEXT` + `test_run_at TEXT` (additive nullable `ALTER`s) — vs. a `attempt_question_runs` table (a row per run, if a learner can run the tests multiple times and you want the history — but `attempt_questions` already has one row per Question per Attempt, so a "latest run" denormalized onto it is simpler unless run-history is wanted). The architect's forecast leans **denormalized columns on `attempt_questions`** (the "latest run result" is what the take surface and the grading slice need; run-history is not a manifest requirement).
- **`is_correct`'s source:** set by the runner (tests passed ≡ correct) — vs. set by the grading slice reading the test result. The architect's forecast leans **set by the grading slice** (keeps the grading slice's "compute the Grade" job — per-Question correctness *and* explanation *and* the aggregate — coherent; the runner produces the raw test result, the grading slice reads it and adds the explanation; §8 Grade's "correctness determined by whether the code passed the tests" is satisfied either way).
- **Displaying the test suite read-only:** show it (a `.quiz-take-test-suite` `<pre>` block per Question — §6's "running tests against it" reads as the learner knowing what the tests are) — vs. hide it (implement from the `prompt` alone). The architect's forecast leans **show it** (you can't meaningfully run tests against your code without knowing what they test; the `prompt` already names the signature per ADR-040, so the tests aren't a *surprise*, they're the *spec*).

## Constraints

- **Manifest §6:** "Code is written, run, and tested within the application. The learner answers a Question by writing code, running it, and running tests against it — all inside the application's own interface. No external editor, no separate test runner, no copy-paste-into-another-tool workflow." — the runner is *in-app*; the learner doesn't leave the application to run tests. This reads as wanting a responsive write→run→see loop.
- **Manifest §5:** "No remote deployment / hosted product" — bounds the sandbox's complexity ceiling (no cloud-execution service; a local sandbox). "No multi-user features" — no `user_id` on the test-result columns. "No coverage of CS-300 Chapters 1–2" / "No LMS features" — orthogonal.
- **Manifest §7 / MC-8:** the test results feed `attempt_questions.is_correct` (the wrong-answer-replay history the composition slice reads) and, via the grading slice, the Weak Topics (the fresh-portion driver). The runner is the source of the raw correctness signal §8 Grade is now defined in terms of.
- **Manifest §6 / MC-4:** MC-4 binds *AI work* ("AI work is asynchronous from the learner's perspective"). **Running a test suite is not AI work** — it's executing code — so MC-4 does *not* require the test run to be out-of-band. The slice's `/design` should reason this explicitly and not reflexively copy ADR-037's async-generation pattern.
- **Manifest §6 / MC-5:** "AI failures are visible, never fabricated" — orthogonal to the runner (no AI in the run path); but a *runner* failure (a crash, a timeout, a compile error in the test suite) must be surfaced honestly to the learner (a clear "the test run timed out" / "the test suite failed to compile" — not a fabricated "all tests passed" or "all tests failed"), in the same spirit.
- **MC-6 (Lecture source read-only):** the sandbox must not be able to write under `content/latex/` (the learner's code runs in a temp working dir away from it; a `bubblewrap`/jail would `--ro-bind` the corpus or not bind it at all). The runner also must not corrupt `data/notes.db` (it runs in a separate process / sandbox; it doesn't touch the DB — the *route* writes the test result to `attempt_questions` via `app.persistence.*` after the sandbox returns).
- **MC-10 (Persistence boundary):** the new `attempt_questions` test-result columns' DDL goes in `connection.py` (via the `_apply_additive_migrations` `PRAGMA table_info` check, mirroring ADR-037's `generation_error` and ADR-041's `questions.test_suite`); a `save_attempt_test_result(attempt_id, question_id, passed, output, ...)` (or similar) persistence function goes in `app/persistence/quizzes.py`; SQL stays under `app/persistence/`; the route calls only typed public functions.
- **MC-7:** single-user — no `user_id` on the test-result columns or any new construct.
- **ADR-040 / ADR-041:** the test suite the runner executes is `questions.test_suite` (a runnable test-code string); the runner reads it via whatever accessor it adds (ADR-041 §No new accessor this task — the runner slice's `/design` adds `get_question` / `get_test_suite_for_question` / etc.). The `question_gen` prompt (ADR-040) steers the LLM toward emitting test files in the implementation's language (currently C++) — the sandbox must handle whichever languages that prompt produces.
- **ADR-038 / ADR-039:** the take surface (`quiz_take.html.j2`) and the `attempt_questions` rows (created at Attempt start, `response` filled on submit) are consumed; this slice adds the "Run tests" affordance (probably *before* submit — the learner runs tests, iterates, then submits) and the test-result columns; whether running tests is allowed only `in_progress` or also after `submitted` is the slice's call.
- **ADR-008:** any take-surface change (a read-only test-suite block, a "Run tests" button, a results panel) reuses the `quiz-take-*` namespace in `app/static/quiz.css` (ADR-039 §The take-page CSS); any per-Section `ready`-entry caption change reuses `section-quiz-*` in `lecture.css`.
- **`pyproject.toml`:** if the sandbox needs a dependency (a `psutil` for process limits, a Docker SDK, none if a bare-subprocess approach) — the slice's `/design` decides; the implementer makes the `pyproject.toml` edit (flagged, not made by the architect — ADR-036's precedent).

## Resolution

When the in-app-test-runner slice's `/design` runs, mark this issue `Resolved by ADR-NNN` (the sandbox/execution ADR + the take-surface/route ADR + the `attempt_questions` test-result-columns persistence ADR it produces — plausibly 2–3 ADRs, mirroring how TASK-014 split into ADR-036 + ADR-037 and TASK-016 into ADR-040 + ADR-041) and note which sandbox was chosen, whether the test run is synchronous or out-of-band (and the MC-4-doesn't-apply reasoning), the test-result column shape, `is_correct`'s source, whether the test suite is displayed read-only, and the per-Section caption call. If the slice's `/design` discovers the grading slice's shape needs updating (it does — `quiz-grading-slice-shape.md`'s current sketch "the grading workflow gets the Questions + the learner's code; returns per-Question correctness" predates the amendment; correctness is now a test-execution result the runner produces), note that for the grading slice's future `/design` (don't edit `quiz-grading-slice-shape.md` here unless a decision in the runner slice forces it).

## Cross-references

- ADR-040 §The test-suite representation (the runner executes `questions.test_suite`, a runnable test-code string; the representation was picked "runnable, not queryable" *for this slice*), §Scope ("the in-app code-execution / test-runner slice … deferred to [this issue]"), §My recommendation vs the user's apparent preference (the conformant order: Questions-carry-tests → in-app test-runner → grading → composition → TTS; why the roadmap memory's order is stale)
- ADR-041 §No new accessor this task (the runner slice's `/design` adds whatever accessor reads `questions.test_suite` at runtime), §Scope ("the in-app code-execution / test-runner slice — the sandbox/isolation, the 'Run tests' route + affordance, the `attempt_questions` test-result columns … deferred to that slice's `/design`")
- ADR-038 §The take surface (`quiz_take.html.j2` — the per-Question `prompt` + `<textarea>`; this slice adds the "Run tests" affordance), §The take-page CSS (`quiz.css`, `quiz-take-*` namespace — ADR-039)
- ADR-039 §The function set (the `attempt_questions` rows created at Attempt start; `response` filled on submit; this slice adds the test-result columns + a `save_attempt_test_result`-style function), §The `AttemptQuestion` dataclass (`is_correct`/`explanation` NULL until grading — this slice's test-result columns are the new sibling fields)
- ADR-033 §Table set (`attempt_questions` — `response`/`is_correct`/`explanation`; the test-result columns are additive — ADR-022's migration story), §Lifecycle enums (`quiz_attempts.status` — does running tests change the status? probably not, it's a within-`in_progress` action — the slice's call), §n-tables-threshold (a `attempt_question_runs` table, if run-history is wanted, might tip the threshold — the slice weighs it)
- ADR-037 §The async out-of-band processor (the *generation* pattern — which this slice should *not* reflexively copy, since running tests is not AI work and MC-4 doesn't bind it; named here so the slice's `/design` consciously chooses synchronous-with-timeout vs out-of-band rather than copying), §Failure-handling-discipline (the `_apply_additive_migrations` `generation_error`-column precedent the test-result columns mirror)
- ADR-022 §Migration story (the test-result columns are additive nullable `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN`s), §Persistence boundary (the columns' DDL in `connection.py`; the `save_attempt_test_result`-style function in `quizzes.py`; SQL stays under `app/persistence/`)
- ADR-008 §Future-surfaces (any take-surface change reuses `quiz-take-*` in `quiz.css`; any `ready`-entry caption change reuses `section-quiz-*` in `lecture.css`)
- `design_docs/project_issues/quiz-grading-slice-shape.md` (the grading slice — its current sketch predates the amendment; correctness is now a test-execution result the runner produces; the grading slice's `/design` updates that issue, not this one)
- `app/templates/quiz_take.html.j2`, `app/static/quiz.css` (the take surface this slice modifies), `app/persistence/quizzes.py` + `connection.py` (where the test-result columns + function go), `app/main.py` (where the "Run tests" route goes), `app/persistence/__init__.py` (re-exports the new function)
- Manifest §6 ("Code is written, run, and tested within the application"), §5 ("No remote deployment"), §7 / §8 (Quiz Attempt "the in-app test results for each response"; Grade correctness "determined by whether the learner's code passed the Question's tests")
- The roadmap memory note (`project_roadmap_ide_and_known_issues.md`) — flags this as the "future big feature: in-browser IDE + run-tests-against-learner-code (needs a manifest amendment first ...)"; the manifest amendment has now happened (2026-05-12), so the precondition is met; the memory's stated *order* (grading first) is stale per ADR-040's reasoning
